name: geo-fundamentals
description: Generative Engine Optimization for AI search engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity).
capabilities: Analyze web pages for AI citation readiness
Verify author attribution and publication dates
Detect FAQ sections and schema markup
Score pages on GEO best practices
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged potential issues (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithm) but manual review confirms these are false positives. The skill is documentation about GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) with a read-only Python script that analyzes local HTML/JSX/TSX files. No network requests, no command injection vectors, no cryptographic code present.
valueStatement: This skill helps content creators optimize their web pages for AI-powered search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity to increase citation rates in AI-generated responses.
userTitle: Optimize Content for AI Search Engines
limitations: Only analyzes HTML, JSX, and TSX files
Does not modify or write files

useCases.title: Web Content Audit
useCases.description: Run the geo_checker.py script on a project to analyze public-facing pages for AI optimization gaps.

useCases.title: SEO Team Workflow
useCases.description: Integrate GEO analysis into existing SEO workflows to ensure content is ready for AI engines.

useCases.title: Pre-Publish Review
useCases.description: Check new web pages for GEO elements before publishing to maximize AI citation potential.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze My Website
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the geo-fundamentals skill to audit my website at [PROJECT_PATH]. Check for structured data, author info, dates, and FAQ sections.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want to check if your web pages are optimized for AI citation

promptTemplates.title: GEO Score Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the geo_checker.py script to get an average GEO score for my project. What elements are missing?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick score of your content optimization level

promptTemplates.title: Improve AI Citations
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on the GEO audit results, suggest specific changes to improve AI citation rates for my content.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want actionable recommendations to boost AI visibility

promptTemplates.title: Check Structured Data
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify if my web pages contain proper JSON-LD structured data for Article, FAQPage, and Organization schemas.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want to verify technical SEO elements

bestPractices: Include structured data (JSON-LD) with Article and FAQPage schemas
Add clear author attribution with credentials
Use question-based headings and direct answer patterns
antiPatterns: Publishing content without dates or author info
Using thin content without original data or statistics
Skipping structured data markup for AI engines
Failing to add FAQ sections for direct answer extraction

faq.question: What is GEO?
faq.answer: GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is the practice of optimizing content for AI-powered search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity to increase the likelihood of being cited in AI-generated responses.

faq.question: What does geo_checker.py analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes HTML, JSX, and TSX files for GEO elements including structured data, author info, publication dates, FAQ sections, and heading structure.

faq.question: Does this skill write or modify files?
faq.answer: No, the geo_checker.py script is read-only. It only reads and analyzes files without making any modifications.

faq.question: What is a good GEO score?
faq.answer: A score of 60% or above is considered passing. Scores above 80% indicate excellent optimization for AI citation.

faq.question: Which AI engines does this support?
faq.answer: The skill targets optimization for ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini - the major AI-powered search engines.

faq.question: Does blocking AI crawlers affect GEO?
faq.answer: Yes, if you block AI crawlers (like GPTBot or Claude-Web) in robots.txt, your content will not be indexed by those AI engines and cannot be cited in responses.
